content.problemStatement:
Chef Sports conducted a fan poll to find out who their fans thought was the best captain.

The three players nominated were Dhoni, Rohit, and Kohli. They received A,B,A, B, A,B, and CCC votes, respectively.

Find out whether Dhoni won the poll, i.e, if he received the maximum number of votes.

**Note:** It is guaranteed that no two players received the same number of votes.


Input Format
The only line of input contains three space-separated integers AAA, BBB, and CCC — the number of votes obtained by Dhoni, Rohit, and Kohli, respectively.



Output Format
Print the answer on a single line: `"Yes"` (without quotes) if Dhoni won the poll, and `"No"` (without quotes) otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `No`, `no`, nO`, and `NO` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: A, B and C are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C — the votes received by Dhoni, Rohit and Kohli respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing "Yes" if Dhoni received strictly more votes than both Rohit and Kohli, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 50 > 23 and 50 > 28 → Yes.\nTest case 2: 25 is not greater than 26 → No.\nTest case 3: 10 is not greater than 20 → No.
First case Dhoni has the smallest vote count → No.\nSecond case Dhoni has the largest vote count → Yes.
Dhoni's 500 votes are less than Rohit's 1000 → No.

solution.approach: For each test case read the three vote counts. Since all three numbers are distinct, Dhoni wins exactly when A > B and A > C. Compare the values and print the appropriate answer. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
